item.text: I’ll read the project guideline entry point first, then submit the structured done artifact exactly through the validator.

item.aggregated_output:
# GUIDELINES

1. See common [RUST.md](./DNA/languages/RUST.md) guideline
2. When develop new REST API use [API.md](./DNA/REST/API.md), [STATUS_CODES](./DNA/REST/STATUS_CODES.md)
3. When develop new Module use [ModKit Unified System](../docs/modkit_unified_system/README.md)
4. Check [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) for security best practices.
5. Review [DEPENDENCIES.md](./DEPENDENCIES.md) for preferred dependencies and libraries.

arguments.summary_title: bug5 resource-group membership type-code validation fixed
arguments.summary_markdown:
## Summary
bug5 was a contract divergence in `cyberware-resource-group`: `TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` validated `allowed_membership_types` with the resource-group type-registry validator, which forced the `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~` prefix onto membership resource type codes. Per the design evidence, allowed membership types are external domain resource types such as `gts.cf.core.idp.user.v1~`, so they must not be constrained to the RG type namespace.

## Fix
The fix introduced and applied membership-specific type-code validation for `allowed_membership_types`. That validation still rejects empty entries and codes longer than 1024 characters, but no longer enforces the RG namespace prefix. Existing RG-prefixed codes remain accepted for backwards compatibility. RG type codes and parent type codes still use the stricter RG `validate_type_code`, and `add_membership` / `remove_membership` behavior was left unchanged.

## Validation
Validated with package-scoped Cargo commands in `/Users/brad/code/gears-rust/.worktrees/marathon-bug5-gpt`: `cargo build -p cyberware-resource-group`, `cargo test -p cyberware-resource-group --test domain_unit_test`, `cargo test -p cyberware-resource-group --test type_service_test`, and the focused `external_allowed_membership_type` acceptance filter. The relevant tests passed, including create/update acceptance for external membership types and rejection coverage for empty and over-length entries. Final `git status --short` was clean.

## Run Cost
There were 0 refinement cycles. The only noted friction was an earlier invalid workspace command pattern (`./...`), which was a Cargo invocation issue rather than a product regression.

## Future Approach
For similar bugs, distinguish registry-owned type identifiers from referenced external domain type identifiers before reusing namespace validators. Validator reuse should be checked against the contract role of the field, not just the fact that the field contains a type-like code.

lessons.description: `allowed_membership_types` looked like a type-code field but represented references to external domain resource types, so applying the RG registry namespace validator created a contract divergence. Similar fixes should first classify whether a type code is registry-owned or externally referenced before reusing prefix-enforcing validation.

The package-scoped Cargo commands were the reliable validation path for this workspace; the earlier `./...` pattern failure was an invalid command shape, while `cargo build -p cyberware-resource-group` and targeted package tests passed cleanly.
